message:
feat(block,btree,journal,fuse,conformance): add Bw-Tree, per-core WAL buffers, I/O throttle injection, and full conformance gate

Core data structures:
- ffs-btree: add bw_tree module implementing safe (no-unsafe) Bw-Tree with
  mapping-table indirection, CAS delta-chain append, crossbeam-epoch deferred
  reclamation, auto-consolidation at configurable threshold, and iterator support.
  Add crossbeam-epoch dependency and criterion bench harness to Cargo.toml.
- ffs-journal: add wal_buffer module implementing Silo/Aether-style per-core
  WAL buffers with thread-local append (lock-free hot path), global epoch
  counter for cross-core ordering, configurable flush watermarks, and group
  commit coalescing via condvar notification. Add parallel WAL E2E tests.

Block layer improvements:
- ffs-block: fix FaultInjector::check_fault to drop mutex guard before
  performing side-effect work (avoids holding lock across sequence counter
  increment and error construction). Remove stale early-return in
  ArcState::on_miss s3fifo feature gate.
- ffs-block: add ThrottleConfig and IoThrottle wrapper for injecting
  configurable read/write latency, bandwidth limiting, and stochastic stalls
  into any BlockDevice — enables degraded-I/O testing without real disks.

Test and conformance:
- ffs-harness: add full_conformance_gate_pass test exercising all 12 fixture
  parsers, checksum manifests, golden references, fuzz corpus, and parity
  report in a single deterministic sub-60s CI gate.
- ffs-fuse: split read_only mount-option test into two focused assertions,
  reformat long assert_eq! calls per rustfmt conventions.
- Update .beads/issues.jsonl and Cargo.lock.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
